channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: In the AI era, data is gold. And these companies are striking it rich
item.description: As businesses scramble to take advantage of artificial intelligence, they’re finding data makes all the difference in using AI effectively.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Software stocks may be primed for a big Wall Street comeback
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Technology Letter and is republished here with permission. It’s been a rough year for software stocks, and a very good year for hardware names such as Nvidia, which just hit a new all-time high, briefly, last week. Maybe it’s time to switch.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft AI CEO claims any content published online is "freeware" to help train AI — and people are not happy
item.description: Although AI is trained on data published online, Microsoft AI CEO still sees the need for greater governance.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Alibaba Cloud closes some key data centers as it pivots to different markets
item.description: Sydney and Mumbai data centers will close this year as Alibaba Cloud focuses on Southeast Asia and new Mexico location.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Upgrade Your Playlists with Apple Music's New Smart Crossfade Feature in iOS 18
item.description: Apple Music is set to receive a smart crossfade feature in iOS 18, allowing users to seamlessly transition between songs on their playlists for an enhanced listening experience.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Unlocking Hybrid Success: Why Small Businesses Need AI-Enhanced PCs
item.description: As hybrid work becomes the norm, small businesses must leverage digital strategies to enable seamless collaboration, and AI-enhanced PCs can play a crucial role in their success.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What key features underpin best-in-class AI PCs?
item.description: As businesses seek to leverage AI in all aspects of their operations, it’s vital that IT leaders understand how this transformative moment will impact hardware. CIOs who aren’t actively investigating the possibilities risk being left behind.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: European Union accuses Facebook owner Meta of breaking digital rules with paid ad-free option
item.description: European Union regulators have accused social media company Meta Platforms of breaching the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Autonomous trash-gobbling robot boat wages war on waterway waste
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides the details on the Class 3 Clearbot: an autonomous vessel for efficient marine trash/plant removal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: InfoWorld Technology of the Year Awards 2024 Nominations Now Open
item.description: The 2024 InfoWorld Technology of the Year Awards will recognize the best and most innovative products across 26 categories in software development, cloud computing, data analytics, and AI and machine learning. Nominations for the 2024 awards are now open.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cities most targeted for travel booking scams
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson takes a look at the five travel destinations most frequently targeted by cybercriminals through online booking scams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DNA Technology Tracks Dog Waste Littering: Residents Required to Submit DNA Samples
item.description: In an effort to curb dog waste littering, apartment complexes and homeowners associations are turning to DNA technology to identify responsible pet owners. The initiative involves collecting DNA samples from dogs to track who is leaving behind piles of waste.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI-powered Avatars of Instagram Creators on the Horizon
item.description: Meta's upcoming 'AI' feature will tag AI-generated avatars with creators' names, marking a new era in digital representation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: FCC proposes rule requiring carriers to unlock all cellphones
item.description: The FCC wants carriers to unlock cellphones within 60 days of purchase so users can shop different networks.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, The Hill

item.title: Too many passwords to remember? Here's what you can do
item.description: According to some unscientific studies, the average person has hundreds of passwords. That's a lot to keep track of.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Opinion: Big tech companies are burning through reservoirs of trust with AI
item.description: Is generative AI a useful productivity tool or a series of overhyped, intrusive products that consume vital resources?
item.dc:creator: Irina Raicu, The Mercury News

item.title: Amazon Investigates Allegations of Unapproved Content Scraping by Perplexity AI
item.description: Amazon is examining claims that Perplexity AI, an AI startup, may be scraping online content, including from prominent news sources, without proper authorization.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Photos: Tech exec’s Pescadero mansion listed for $8 million
item.description: Features of the 6,000-square-foot mansion include a two-story solarium and an indoor swimming pool with underwater channel to the outdoor lap pool.
item.dc:creator: Pueng Vongs, The Mercury News

item.title: Transforming Healthcare: Insights on Tomorrow's Trends
item.description: Explore the latest developments in healthcare transformation, measurement, and integration, shaping the future of patient care and medical innovation.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: State Department Emphasizes AI Experimentation with New Tools
item.description: The State Department encourages employees to test new AI tools like State Chat and North Star, while sharing their own use cases to shape the agency's AI strategy.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Detroit Settles $300K with Man Wrongly Accused of Theft, Updates Facial Recognition Practices
item.description: The city of Detroit has agreed to pay $300,000 to a man who was wrongly accused of theft due to errors in its facial recognition technology. As part of the settlement, the city is also making changes to its use of the technology.
item.dc:creator: Ed White, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: White House to hold conference for digital creators
item.description: The Biden administration will host digital creators at the White House for the first-ever Creator Economy Conference in August, the director of digital strategy announced Friday. The conference, which will be held Aug.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Massachusetts Secures $175 Million Settlement with Uber, Lyft for Fair Treatment of Drivers
item.description: Uber and Lyft agree to provide minimum wage, paid sick leave, and other benefits to drivers in Massachusetts as part of a landmark settlement
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Enterprise buyer’s guide: Android smartphones for business
item.description: Android dominates smartphone usage throughout the world — in every region except North America and Oceania. Thus, businesses in many regions are likely to support and issue Android devices to employees as their mainstay mobile devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Doxxing victims in California may gain right to sue to ‘bring some power back’
item.description: A state bill would allow doxxing victims to sue those responsible in civil court for as much as $30,000, plus attorneys’ fees and court costs. Supporters say it’s needed to stem online harassment against LGBTQ residents and others.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: AI success: Real or hallucination?
item.description: Ever consider how much of AI is a hallucination? Not how much AI output is hallucination, but rather how much of what we hear or read about it is genuine? Could it be that AI success is mostly hype, and that all the hype may be hurting the technology rather than helping? Enterprises need to get to t
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Supreme Court rules for Biden administration in a social media dispute with conservative states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday sided with the Biden administration in a dispute with Republican-led states over how far the federal government can go to combat controversial social media posts on topics including COVID-19 and election security.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Broadcom bolsters VMware Edge Compute Stack
item.description: A slew of updates in VMware Edge Compute Stack (ECS) 3.5 are aimed at helping customers more easily manage edge devices, applications, and infrastructure across multiple locations, and industry watchers say the updated platform will support AI at the edge.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why NASA astronauts are delayed at the space station after Boeing Starliner launch
item.description: Two astronauts are spending extra time at the International Space Station as NASA and Boeing troubleshoot capsule problems that popped up on the way there.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Vatican Radio territory in north Rome once blamed for electro-smog will become a solar farm
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis decreed Wednesday that an area of northern Rome, long the source of controversy because of electromagnetic waves emitted by Vatican Radio towers there, will now house a field of solar panels to fuel Vatican City. Citing the Vatican’s pledge in U.N.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Philanthropic Trends in Silicon Valley: Insights from CEO Nicole Taylor
item.description: As President and CEO of the Silicon Valley Community Foundation, Nicole Taylor shares her expertise on philanthropic trends and their intersection with technology and politics.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Stability AI Seeks Revival with New CEO and Investor Backing
item.description: The AI image-generation company behind Stable Diffusion, facing challenges, appoints a new CEO and secures investment from a group featuring Sean Parker, co-founder of Napster and former Facebook president.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia won't pay an $8 million ransom after a cyberattack compromised its national data center
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesia's national data center has been compromised by a hacking group asking for a $8 million ransom that the government says it won't pay.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia Resists $8M Ransom Demand After National Data Center Cyberattack
item.description: Jakarta, Indonesia - A hacking group compromised the country's national data center, demanding an $8 million ransom that Indonesia has refused to pay, highlighting its commitment to cybersecurity and data protection.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Car dealerships in North America revert to pens and paper after cyberattacks on software provider
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Car dealerships in North America are still wrestling with major disruptions that started last week with cyberattacks on a company whose software is used widely in the auto retail sales sector. CDK Global, a company that provides software for thousands of auto dealers in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Music record labels sue AI song-generators Suno and Udio for copyright infringement
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Big record companies are suing artificial intelligence song-generators Suno and Udio for copyright infringement, alleging that the AI music startups are exploiting the recorded works of artists from Chuck Berry to Mariah Carey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple becomes first target of EU's new digital competition rules aimed at big tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators on Monday leveled their first charges under the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook, accusing Apple of preventing app makers from pointing users to cheaper options outside its App Store.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
